one_line: Major-General commanding the British Central India Field Force in 1858.

appearance.age_look: Early 50s, medium height, lean but solid build with a military bearing.
appearance.face: Long oval face with high forehead, pronounced cheekbones, aquiline nose, neatly trimmed grey moustache without beard, pale weathered complexion, sharp blue-grey eyes; default expression is calm, stern focus with a slight furrowed brow.
appearance.hair: Receding straight grey hair, combed back and short at the sides, visible under a dark blue British Army general’s peaked forage cap with a black patent leather peak, narrow red band, and small brass regimental badge centered above the peak.
appearance.costume: Mid-19th-century British Army general’s undress frock coat in dark navy wool, single-breasted with a standing collar edged in scarlet piping, brass buttons in a single row, and plain shoulder straps; scarlet cuffs with thin gold piping, plain khaki drill trousers with a faintly darker khaki stripe down the side, and polished black leather ankle boots with slight square toes; around his waist a dark brown leather sword belt with a simple brass buckle, canting slightly down on the left hip; over his right shoulder a light khaki canvas field satchel strap crossing the chest; no parade braid or medals, just a practical campaign look with slightly dust-marked hems.
appearance.signature: A British pattern 1822 infantry officer’s sword with a gilt three-bar guard and slightly curved steel blade, carried in a plain steel scabbard hanging from his left hip on the leather belt, his gloved right hand often resting lightly on the hilt as if ready to draw.

ref_desc: Sir Hugh Rose is a stern blue-grey-eyed British general with receding grey hair under a dark navy peaked cap, wearing a dark navy frock coat with scarlet piping, khaki trousers, black boots, a brown sword belt, and carrying a brass-guard 1822 officer’s sword in a steel scabbard, with navy, scarlet, khaki, black, and brass tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Sir Hugh Rose, a mid-19th-century British general: early 50s, lean, long oval face, high forehead, pale weathered skin, aquiline nose, neat grey moustache, sharp blue-grey eyes, calm stern expression with a slight brow furrow. His receding straight grey hair is combed back, short at the sides, and mostly hidden beneath a dark navy British general’s peaked forage cap with a black patent leather peak, a narrow red band, and a small brass badge centrally above the peak. He wears a dark navy wool single-breasted frock coat with a standing collar edged in scarlet piping, a single row of brass buttons, plain shoulder straps, and scarlet cuffs with thin gold piping; the coat shows slight dust at the lower edges to reflect field service. He has plain khaki drill trousers with a subtly darker khaki side stripe and polished black leather ankle boots with slightly square toes. Around his waist is a dark brown leather sword belt with a simple brass buckle, hanging a steel scabbard on his left hip; a light khaki canvas satchel strap crosses from his left shoulder to his right hip. His signature weapon is a British pattern 1822 infantry officer’s sword with a gilt three-bar guard and gently curved steel blade, resting in the scabbard with his right gloved hand lightly on the hilt as if ready to draw. Emphasize anime-style linework but historically grounded details, and use a palette dominated by dark navy blue, scarlet red accents, khaki cloth, black leather, and brass metal.
